name: Spear of Ares
description: The Spear of Ares, a weapon of immense power and divine origin, is intrinsically linked to the Greek god of war, Ares. Forged in the heart of Hephaestus's volcanic forges, it is said to be imbued with the very essence of battle and bloodshed. The spear is not merely a weapon of destruction; it amplifies Ares's own formidable abilities, granting him unparalleled strength, speed, and ferocity on the battlefield. Its presence alone incites chaos and inspires terror in the hearts of mortals and gods alike. The spear is often depicted as being crafted from adamant, an indestructible metal, and adorned with imagery symbolic of war, such as skulls, blood, and snarling beasts. Legends claim that when the Spear of Ares strikes the ground, it can unleash earthquakes or summon armies of undead warriors. Its power is so great that only Ares himself can wield it without being consumed by its bloodlust. Though often associated with destruction, the spear is also a symbol of Ares's authority and dominion over warfare. The loss of the Spear of Ares would signify a weakening of Ares's power and a potential shift in the balance of power among the Olympian gods. The spear’s influence is felt across countless battles in Greek mythology, often turning the tide of war in favor of those aligned with Ares. It is a constant reminder of the brutal and unforgiving nature of conflict, embodying the raw, untamed power of the god of war.
subtitle: Weapon of War, Embodiment of Chaos
currentLocation: Unknown, presumed in Ares's possession or lost on a battlefield

keyMyths.title: Ares's Battles
keyMyths.description: The Spear of Ares is frequently mentioned in accounts of Ares's various battles, where it serves as his primary weapon and a symbol of his destructive power. Its presence often turns the tide of battle.

keyMyths.title: The Forging of the Spear
keyMyths.description: Hephaestus, the god of the forge, crafted the Spear of Ares using adamant and imbued it with the essence of war, making it a weapon of unparalleled power. This act solidified the spear's importance to Ares.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Mentions Ares's involvement in the Trojan War and his use of powerful weapons.
sources.text: Mentions Ares's involvement in the Trojan War and his use of powerful weapons.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Provides details about the creation of divine weapons and the Olympian gods.
